gate_lock: gates.yaml gate_2 locked (ratified PR #79 merge 82006877 + flip #81); protocol/views/tolerances read at runtime, no threshold moved.

secondary_failure: remarriage.after_divorce (smoothing bias in thin late bands)

model.class: stratified empirical family-transition hazards with a mortality-composed widowhood component

components.first_marriage: discrete-time logistic hazard; natural cubic (restricted) spline on age, knots 20/25/30/40 (3 basis columns), sex, birth-decade cohort main effects + age-spline x cohort interaction; sklearn LogisticRegression(penalty='l2', C=1.0, lbfgs) fit with sample_weight = person-year PSID weight (effectively the weighted MLE at train scale); design standardised for conditioning
components.divorce: weighted empirical hazard by marriage-duration band x marriage order (1st vs 2+), add-one (Laplace) smoothed at the train mean married person-year weight
components.widowhood: COMPOSED: train age-band x sex mortality hazard (build_mortality_floors construction, train persons) applied to the spouse (opposite sex, age = self age + train mean spousal age gap by sex); widowhood = induced transition
components.remarriage: weighted empirical hazard by years-since-dissolution band x origin (divorced/widowed) x sex, add-one smoothed at the train mean dissolved person-year weight
components.fertility: weighted empirical age-band x parity (0/1/2/3+) rates by birth-decade cohort, train-estimated (no smoothing)

registered_ambiguity_resolutions.logistic_estimator: 'discrete-time logistic hazard fit' = MLE; sklearn LogisticRegression default L2 (C=1.0) is negligible vs the weighted data term at ~2-3e5 train person-years, so the fit is effectively the weighted MLE while remaining numerically stable on thin cohorts (statsmodels absent; sklearn 1.9)
registered_ambiguity_resolutions.spline: natural cubic = restricted cubic spline (Harrell), 4 knots -> 3 basis columns, linear beyond the boundary knots
registered_ambiguity_resolutions.table_weighting: empirical tables estimated WEIGHTED (person-year PSID weight), consistent with the weighted gated statistics
registered_ambiguity_resolutions.add_one_smoothing: Laplace: one pseudo-event + one pseudo-non-event at the train cell-universe mean person-year weight -- (Wnum + w) / (Wden + 2w); negligible on populated cells
registered_ambiguity_resolutions.competing_risks: married-year single uniform: divorce if u < p_div, widowhood if p_div <= u < p_div + p_wid, else stays married
registered_ambiguity_resolutions.initial_state: each holdout person's observed marital state at their entry person-year (41,346 never_married / 62 married / 1 separated at entry); simulation begins from it

protocol.split: populace_dynamics.harness.panel.split_panel_by_person(panel.attrs, 'person_id', fraction=0.5, seed=s); side A = the holdout, side B = the train complement
protocol.scored_against: side A's own empirical rate (rate_a in runs/gate2_floors_v2.json noise_floor_per_seed)
protocol.conjunction: all 46 gated cells per seed AND >= 4 of 5 gate seeds
protocol.weight_definition: person-constant most-recent positive PSID cross-sectional weight (populace_dynamics.data.panels.demographic_panel); every gated statistic weighted, none unweighted

precheck.note: Hard-stop precheck (gate-1 mirror): the scoring path must reproduce every committed full-panel reference moment and every committed per-gate-seed rate_a bit-for-bit, and each gate seed's holdout-id sha256, before any candidate is simulated.

report_only.note: The 16 report-only cells (below the 20-event floor, above the T_max power cap, or superseded by a gating aggregate). Scored with the same |ln(r_candidate / rate_a)| statistic and published; never gated.

verdict.rule: A seed passes iff every one of the 46 gated cells holds (|ln(r_candidate / rate_a)| <= locked tolerance); the gate passes iff >= 4 of the 5 gate seeds pass.
